topics.title: Variables
topics.definition: A variable is a named reference used to store data in memory. Variables allow programs to store, retrieve, and manipulate values during execution.
topics.code:
name='Pooja'
age=28
salary=50000.50
is_active=True

print(name)
print(age)
topics.tip: Python is dynamically typed. Variables can change type during runtime.
topics.keywords: variables dynamic typing memory reference variable naming rules multiple assignment

topics.title: Data Types
topics.definition: Data types define the type of value stored in a variable. Common Python data types include int, float, str, bool, list, tuple, set, and dictionary.
topics.code:
age = 28
salary = 50000.50
name = 'Pooja'
is_active = True

print(type(age))
topics.tip: Understanding data types is essential before learning operators and control statements.
topics.keywords: data types int float string bool type isinstance

topics.title: Arithmetic Operators
topics.definition: Arithmetic operators are used to perform mathematical calculations.
topics.code:
a = 10
b = 5

print(a + b)
print(a - b)
print(a * b)
print(a / b)
topics.tip: Remember the difference between / and // in interviews.
topics.keywords: arithmetic operators addition subtraction multiplication division modulus
